ety: inf. n. v of فوق 'to be superior (to)'
def: s.m. Exalting oneself (above others), pretending to a superiority (over others); superiority, ascendancy; — doing (anything) leisurely or by degrees.
more: (inf. v. of فوق) s. m. 1. Pretending to a superiority over others. 2. Doing any thing by degrees.

ety: inf. n. v of فأل; but formed from fāl, q.v.
def: s.m. Auguring good (by or from), regarding as a good omen; taking an omen (from); presaging happily; augury
more: (inf. v. of فال) s. m. Taking an omen (especially a good one from the word of another).

ety: inf. n. v of فضل 'to exceed, to excel'
def: s.m. Excess; excelling; deserving; conferring a benefit (on); beneficence, kindness, favour.
more: (inf. v. of فضل) s. m. 1. Beneficence, kindness, deserving. 2. Excelling, excess. (Plur. تفضلات).

ety: act. part. of طفح 'to be full so as to overflow'
def: part. adj. Full to overflowing (a vessel, river, &c.), running over; full of wine, intoxicated.
more: (part. act. of طفح) adj. Overflowing, full of drink.

ety: inf. n. ii of فہم
def: s.f. Making (one) understand or know; teaching instructing, informing.
(inf. ii. of فـﮩم) s. f. Teaching, instructing, informing.

ety: for A. تعفية, inf. n. ii of عفو 'to become effaced'
def: s.f. Effacing, obliteration; amending, adjusting.
s.m. Pride; affected magnificence.
s. (dakh.) Blow, slap, box on the ear ( Harris ).

ety: inf. n. ii of فرد 'to be or become single or alone'
def: s.f. Separation from others, retirement from the world (esp. for the sake of devotion); the being unique in science, &c.
more: (inf. ii. of فرد) s. f. Separation from others, retirement from the world (especially, for the sake of devotion), the being unique in science, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرح 'to be glad'
def: s.f. Rejoicing, gladdening, exhilarating; gratification; diversion, amusement, recreation; fun, jest
more: n. f. A. فرح expanding. 1. Rejoicing; diversion; exhilaration. 2. Recreation; taking the air, or a holiday. 3. Jest ( mazāq ). tafrīh-i-tabā , n. m. See تفريح (1.) Cheerfulness; hilarity. tafrīh k . v. n. 1. To refresh or divert oneself. 2. To laugh and jest. tafrī'han , adv. 1. For the sake of recreation. 2. In jest; humourously ( haṅsī se ).
(inf. ii. of فرح) s. f. Rejoicing, exhilarating, amusement, gratification. تفريح طبع tafrīḥ-i-t̤abﻌ, Ease of mind, cheerfulness, hilarity.
subs: s.f. Diversion of mind; ease of mind; cheerfulness, hilarity
v.n. To divert or amuse oneself, to take recreation; to make fun, to be cheerful, to laugh and joke.

ety: acc. of tafrīḥ
def: adv. By way of diversion or amusement, for the sake of recreation; in fun, in jest.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرق; see tafriqa
def: s.f. Separating, dividing; distinguishing, discriminating; separation, division; analysis; distribution, partition, participation; distinction, difference, variety; classification; class, department; (in Arith.) subtraction; — jealousy, suspicion; misunderstanding
more: n. f. 1. Partition; severalty division; division of land ( baṭwāṛā ). 2. Classification; class; department. 3. Distinction; difference; variety (انتر). 4. ( Math .) Subtraction. tafrīq k. , v. n. 1. To distinguish; separate; specify; define; classify. 2. ( In law ) To sever; partition; allot. 3. To subtract; deduct; take away.
(inf. ii. of فرق) s. f. 1. Separation, division, analysis. 2. Partition, participation. 3. Jealousy, misunderstanding. 4. Department. تفريق کرنا tafrīḳ karnā, To analyze.
subs: (-kī), To separate; to analyse; to divide, partition, allot; to distinguish, discriminate, specify, define; to classify; to subtract
s.m. A deed specifying the shares allotted to different parties.

ety: for A. تفرقة, tafriqat, fem., inf. n. ii of فرق 'to make a difference (between)'
def: s.m. Difference, distinction; separation, division; variance, discord, disunion (syn. phūṭ); scattering, dispersion; distress; divorce
more: (inf. ii. of فرق) s. m. 1. Separation, dispersion, distinction. 2. Distress. 3. Divorce. (Plur. تفاريق). تفرقه کرنا tafriḳa karnā, To separate, to distinguish.
subs: (-meṅ), To sow discord (between), to set at variance, to separate
(-kā), To separate, divide; to distinguish, discriminate; to scatter, disperse.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرس 'to break the neck (of an animal)'
def: s.f. Breaking or tearing in pieces, rending.
more: (inf. ii. of فرس Heb. He broke) s. f. Rending, breaking or tearing in pieces.

ety: perf. part. of tafs-īdan; rt. Zend tafś, for tap = S. तप्
def: part. adj. Warmed, heated, burning; burnt; cracked or chapped (lips).
more: part. pass. (of تفسيدن) 1. Burning, heated, warmed. 2. Cracked (lips).

ety: inf. n. ii of فصل 'to separate or divide'
def: s.f. Analysis, separation, division; explaining distinctly or in detail; explanation; particulars, details
more: n. m. A. فصل cutting, defining. 1. Details; particulars; minuteparticulars; specification; exposition; explanation. tafsīl-i-hāl , Particulars of a case. ba-tafsīl-i-zail, muffasilah zail , In form and manner following; as follows. tafsīl k., yā likhnā , v. n. To exhibit in detail; enumerate; particularize; explain at full length; specify. tafsīl-vār, tafsīlan , adv. In detail ( bil-tafsīl ); circumstantially; particularly. tafsīl yeh hai . On this wise; to wit. pūrī tafsīl . Full particulars or details.
(inf. ii. of فصل) s. f. Explanation, analysis, separation, division, detail. تفصيل کرنا tafṣīl karnā, To detail. تفصيلوار tafṣīl-wār, adv. Distinctly, in detail.
subs: (-kī), tafṣīl likhnā (-kī), To give the detail (of), exhibit in detail, to detail; to specify, particularize, explain in full or minutely
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَفْصِیل (tafsīl), from Arabic تَفْصِيل (tafṣīl). First attested in c. 1645 as Middle Hindi تفصیل (tfṣyl).

ety: acc. of tafṣīl
def: adv. In detail, &c. = tafṣīl-wār, q.v.s.v. tafṣīl.
formal plural of تَفْصِیل (tafsīl)
direct plural of تَفْصِیل (tafsīl)
oblique plural of تفصیل

ety: inf. n. ii of فسر 'to disclose; to explain'
def: s.f. Explaining, expounding, interpreting; explanation, exposition, interpretation (esp. of the Qorʼān); paraphrase; commentary.
more: n. f. A. فسر he analyzed. Explanation; commentary ( ṭīkā ); an interpretation, especially of the Qurān .
(inf. ii. of فسر Heb. He interpreted) s. f. Explanation, commentary, paraphrase, an interpretation of the ḳurʼān.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَفْسِير (tafsīr).

ety: contrac. of tafta, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Hot, warm; — heat, warmth.

ety: perf. part. of tāftan; Pehl. tāpta; Zend tafta, rt. tap; S. tāpita
def: s.m. lit. 'Twisted; woven; heated,' &c.; a kind of silk, taffeta; a glossy cream colour (as in pigeons and horses).
more: n. m. تافتن A kind of silk; taffetā.
s.m. 1. A kind of silk, taffeta. 2. A colour in pigeons and horses, glossy cream colour.

ety: Zend tafta, rt. tap; S. तप्त, rt. तप्
def: part. adj. Heated; melted; hot, burning; displeased, grieved; twisted; — s.m. Quicklime
more: (for تافته part. pass. of تافتن) 1. adj. Hot, burning, melted. (Plur. تفتگان). 2. s. m. Quick lime.
subs: adj. Displeased or grieved at heart.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Heated state; heat, warmth; ardour, fervour; trouble, anguish, teen.
more: (in compos.) s. f. Heat, ardour, trouble (of mind, &c.).

ety: inf. n. ii of فتش 'to examine,' &c.
def: s.f. Examination, scrutiny, investigation; diligent inquiry or search, research
more: n. f. from فتش examining, searching. Search; scrutiny; diligent investigation; examination.
(inf. ii. of فتش) s. f. Examination, inquiry, search, investigation, research.
subs: (-kī), To look closely (into), to search diligently (after), &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فوض not in use
def: s.f. Committing (to), consigning (to); consignment (of); confiding (to), placing in trust or deposit; handing over (to); forwarding; cession; recommending; — giving (a woman) in marriage without a dowry
more: n. f. A فوض consigning. Committing to another; putting in trust or deposit. tafvīz k . v. n. To make over ( sauṅpnā ); put in deposit or trust. tafvīz-i-māl . Consignment of goods.
(inf. ii. of فوض) s. f. 1. Resigning, committing to another, cession, recommending, confiding to another. 2. Marrying a wife without a fortune.
subs: v.t. To commit (to), consign (to), confide (to), hand over (to), place in trust or deposit, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فضح 'to disgrace'
def: s.f. Exposing the vices or faults (of); putting to shame; disgrace, disrepute, ignominy.
more: (inf. ii. of فضح) s. f. Disgrace, disrepute, ignominy.

ety: inf. n. ii of فضل 'to exceed,' &c.
def: s.f. Excellence, pre-eminence
more: n. f. A فضل increasing. Comparison.
(inf. ii. of فضل) Excellence, preeminence. اسم تفضيل ism-i-tafẓīl, An adjective in the comparative or superlative degree.
subs: An adjective in the comparative or superlative degree

def: ( Gram .) The comparative degree.
n. f. ( Gram .) The superlative degree.
( Gram .) The positive degree.
s.m. The poplar; the elm; the tamarisk; a pomegranate; an ensign-staff.
more: s. m. 1. The poplar. 2. The elm. 3. Tamarisk. 4. A pomegranate. 5. An ensign staff.

ety: Zend taka, rt. tak = S. तक
def: s.f. Running, flying, rushing
subs: tag-ā-pūʼī, tag-ā-dau, tag-tāz, tag-dau, tag-o-pū, tag-o-tāz, or tag-o-dau, s.f. Running about in quest of employment or on business; great exertion or effort; close pursuit or search (after); bustle, toil, fatigue; anxiety, care, concern (syn. dauṛ-dhūp; udheṛ-bun).

ety: S. तन्तु+अग्र+कः; or तन्त्र+कः
def: s.m. Thread, cord (also written dhāgā)
more: s. m. A thread. تاگ توڙ tāg-toṛ, s. m. Lace.
subs: (-meṅ), To put stitches (in), to stitch (anything padded, as a quilt, &c.)
(-kā), To look closely (into or after), to scrutinize; to attend (to), entertain (a guest, &c.); to question closely; to call to account.

def: s.f. = tak, 'a scale.'
more: s. f. Running. See تگ دو.

def: s.m. A poll-tax.

ety: S. तन्तु+अग्र+कः; or तन्त्र+कः
def: s.m. Thread, cord (also written dhāgā)
more: n. m. S. तन्तुक 1. A thread (ḍorā) ; chord. Toṛ ḍāl tāgā, tū kis bhaṛve ke mūṅh lāgā? . Prov. Break off this new attachment, who, what bawd hath snared you in these toils? 2. ( tāgā ) A poll tax. tāgā pironā , v. n. To thread (a needle). āgā tāgā lenā , v. n. 1. To attend to or entertain (a guest). 2. To close question; to be inquisitive. 3. To call to account.
s. m. A thread. تاگ توڙ tāg-toṛ, s. m. Lace.
subs: (-meṅ), To put stitches (in), to stitch (anything padded, as a quilt, &c.)
(-kā), To look closely (into or after), to scrutinize; to attend (to), entertain (a guest, &c.); to question closely; to call to account.

ety: inf. n. vi of غبن 'to deceive, cheat'
def: s.m. Mutual cheating or deception, mutual endeavour to overreach.

ety: inf. n. vi of غفل 'to be unmindful,' &c.
def: s.m. Unmindfulness, heedlessness, forgetfulness, neglect, negligence, inattention, inadvertence, indifference, listlessness (syn. g̠aflat)
more: n. m. غفل neglecting. Ped. for غفلت q. v.

(from غفلت) s. m. 1. Negligence, neglect, indifference, inattention, inadvertency. 2. Being absorbed as in sleep, &c. تغافل شعار tag̠ẖāful-shiʼāﻌr or تغافل کيش tag̠ẖāful-kesh, adj. Careless, inattentive. تغافل شعاري tag̠ẖāful-shiʼāﻌr-ī, s. f. Inattention, carelessness.
subs: (-meṅ), To be unmindful, or neglectful, &c. (in respect of)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَغَافُل (taḡāful).

def: s.f. = tag̠āful.

ety: tagā˚, base of tagānā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका(तिका)
def: s.f. The act of quilting; the price paid for quilting, &c.
(from تگانا) s. f. 1. The price paid for quilting, &c. 2. The act of quilting.
n. f. Quilting, or the charge for it.

ety: inf. n. v of غير; see tag̠īr
def: s.m. Becoming altered or changed; change, alteration; deterioration; removal; becoming vitiated or corrupt; falsification (of documents)

ety: inf. n. v of غلب 'to take or obtain by force'
def: s.m. Taking advantage (of), cheating; imposition; forgery; falsification; embezzlement; breach of trust; — adulteration
more: n. m. from غلب overpowering. Embezzlement; breach of trust; peculation ( gaban ).
(inf. v. of غلب) s. m. Taking advantage, cheating, imposition, forgery, falsification, embezzlement.
subs: v.n. To practise imposition (on), to commit forgery; to embezzle, &c.

def: s.f. = tag̠allub.
more: s. Adulteration. تغلبي کرنا tag̠ẖallub-ī karnā, To adulterate, counterfeit, forge ( Harris ).

def: s.m. A combination of six short syllables.

ety: ta+gaṇa
def: s.m. (in Pros.), The measure called hypo-bacchic or anti-bacchic.

ety: caus. of tagnā
def: v.t. To cause to be quilted; to get stiched together.
more: v. a. Caus. of تگنا q. v.
(caus. of تگنا) v. a. To cause to be quilted, to get stitched together.

ety: inf. n. v of غني 'to be free from want'; 'to be content'
def: s.f. Contentment; singing; cooing; celebrating (a mistress) in verse.
more: (inf. v. of غنا) s. f. Singing, cooing, celebrating a mistress in verse, contentment.

def: s.f. See s.v. tag for compounds of which tag is the first member. s.m. The poplar; the elm; the tamarisk; a pomegranate; an ensign-staff.
more: n. f. Search. See تک ودو
s. f. Bustle, toil, labour, search.

ety: prob. from A. غار
def: s.m. Platter, pail, bucket, tub, trough; a mason's mud or lime pit
more: n. m. Masons' mud or lime pit. tagār bujhānā , v. a. To make mud or lime cement. tagār'ī , n. f. A kneading-trough.
s. m. A tub, bucket, trough, pail, platter.
subs: v.n. To make mortar, or slake lime, in a pit.

def: s.m. The shrub Tabernœmontana coronaria, and a fragrant powder prepared from it.

ety: Prk. टगरो; S. टङ्ग+रः
def: s.m. Borax; — wanton play or sport; wandering of the mind, confusion, bewilderment, perplexity.

ety: cf. tagṛī
def: s.m. A plate or leaf of metal; a medal; a device on a shield (cf. tamg̠a).
s.m. Hail, a hail-storm.
s.f. A small tub or trough, a kneading trough.
more: s. f. A small tub, trough, or bucket.

def: v.n. = ṭagharnā, q.v. v.n. To move or walk to and fro ( = ṭahalnā), to roam, stroll; to roll; to flow; (cf. ḍag; ḍagar; ḍagarnā; ṭahal and ṭahalnā).

ety: inf. n. vi of غير; see g̠air
def: s.m. Difference, diversity, discrepancy.
more: (inf. vi. of غير) s. m. Difference, diversity.

def: s. f. Fatigue, running about on business or in search of employment, great exertion.
v. a. To pursue closely; chase; assault.

ety: caus. from tag = S. tak
def: v.t. To run after, to chase, pursue closely; to assault (cf. rageṛnā).
s.m. The husband of a wet-nurse (syn. dhāʼoṛā).

ety: see tāgā
def: s.m. A strand or thread of a twist; sewing thread
more: n. m. S. तन्तुक. Sewing thread. taggā bāṅdhnā , v. n. To tie a thread on the right wrist in honor of Lakshmī , q. v. The act of tying a thread on the wrist of the /?/ure bahnoī (brother-in-law) by which ceremony the marriage contract is sealed. taggā kholnā , v. n. To untie the thread consecrated to Lakshmī on the 8th day of Kuār .
n. m. The husband of the wet-nurse ( dhāoṛā ).
subs: (-par), To tie a thread (on the right wrist, in honour of Lakshmī).

ety: dim. of taggā, or tāgā, q.v.
def: s.f. A strand or thread of a twist; a kind of fishing line (not used with a rod, prop. ćau-taggī, q.v.).
more: n. f. A strand or thread of a twist. ti-taggī , n. f. A thread of three strands. chau-taggī , n. f. 1. A thread of four strands. 2. A kind of fishing line not used with a rod.
s. f. 1. A strand or thread of a twist. تتگي ti-taggī, A thread of three strands. چو تگي chau-taggī, One of four strands. 2. A kind of fishing-line not used with a rod.

def: v.n. = ṭagharnā, q.v. v.n. To move or walk to and fro ( = ṭahalnā), to roam, stroll; to roll; to flow; (cf. ḍag; ḍagar; ḍagarnā; ṭahal and ṭahalnā).
s. (dakh.) Trough ( Harris ).

ety: ṭaghar˚ = S. त्रख्, or त्रङ्ग्+र
def: v.n. To move or walk to and fro ( = ṭahalnā), to roam, stroll; to roll; to flow; (cf. ḍag; ḍagar; ḍagarnā; ṭahal and ṭahalnā).
v. n. To flow.
(corrupt. of طاغي) adj. Rebellious.
v.t. = ṭaghrānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to move to and fro; to rub (with oil, &c.); to cause to roll, to roll; to cause to flow.
E. v. a. 1. To rub (oil). 2. To cause to flow.

ety: caus. of ṭagharnā
def: v.t. To cause to move to and fro; to rub (with oil, &c.); to cause to roll, to roll; to cause to flow.
s.f. = tagṛī, q.v. s.f. The thread which Hindūs (esp. children) fasten round the waist.
s. f. Removal (from office), deposition.

ety: for A. طاغ t̤āg̠iṉ, act. part. of طغي 'to exceed the just limit'
def: adj. & s.m. Rebellious, refractory, violent; — a rebel, an insurgent.

ety: contrac. of A. تغيير tag̠yīr, inf. n. ii of غير; formed from g̠air, 'other'
def: s.f. Changing, altering (for the worse); change, alteration; removing (from office), discharging, dismissing; discharge, dismissal; — adj. Changed, altered; discharged, dismissed
more: n. m. غير destroying. 1. Revolutionary changes; bouleversement . 2. Falsification or alteration of papers.

(for تغيير inf. ii. of غير) s. f. Alteration, change. adj. Changed, discharged, dismissed. تغيرکرنا tag̠ẖīr karnā, To remove, to dismiss, to discharge (from office), to alter. تغيرهونا tag̠ẖīr honā, v. n. To be altered, removed or dismissed, to alter. ¶ tag̠ẖaiyur (inf. v. ) s. m. Change, alteration, removal.
subs: (with acc. of direct, and abl. of indirect object), To change, alter; to remove, dismiss (a person, from an office)
v.n. To be changed or altered, to alter; to be removed (from office), be dismissed.

ety: for A. tag̠yīrāt, pl. of tag̠yīr
def: s.f. Changes, alterations, &c.
more: plur. of تغير tag̠ẖīr, q. v.

def: s.f. The state of alteration or change; revolution; discharge, dismissal, deposition, disgrace.
more: s. f. The state of alteration or change, revolution, discharge, dismission, disgrace.

ety: from tāgā, q.v.
def: v.t. To stitch a quilt, to quilt ( = tāgā ḍālnā).

ety: from P. tak or tag = S. tak
def: v.t. To cause to run or gallop.
adj. Changed, converted; transposed, &c.
more: adj. قلب converting. Converted; changed; altered.

def: s.m. = tamg̠a, q.v.
more: s. m. A mark which artists put on their productions, device on a shield, arms or armorial bearings, a medal.

ety: from tāg
def: v.t. To thread (a needle).

ety: from tāgā, q.v.
def: v.t. To quilt; to stitch together.
more: v. n. H. tāgā , a thread. To quilt; stitch together.
(from تاگا) v. a. To quilt, to stitch together.

def: s. f. Fatigue, running about on business or in search of employment, great exertion.
s. Search, inquiry, bustle.
s. m. Running, course, gallop.

ety: tag˚ = tak˚ = S. स्तक?+ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. Stout, strong, robust.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀢𑀕𑁆𑀕 (*tagga) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀟- (-ḍa-) + -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit *तर्ग (*targa, “frightening; terrible; strong”), from the root तर्ज् (tarj, “to threaten; scold; frighten”).

ety: S. टगर+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Squint-eyed.

ety: caus. of ṭagarnā
def: v.t. = ṭaghrānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to move to and fro; to rub (with oil, &c.); to cause to roll, to roll; to cause to flow.

ety: tāga, q.v.+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. The thread which Hindūs (esp. children) fasten round the waist.

ety: tāg, q.v.+ṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A thread, or a gold or silver chain, worn round the waist; a girdle, a zone (syn. kardhanī).
n. f. H. tāgā thread. A thread or an ornamental gold or silver chain worn round the waist.

ety: inf. n. ii of غرب 'to go away,' &c.
def: s.f. Removing to a distance; putting away; banishment, transportation: imprisonment.
s.f. = tagāʼī, q.v. s.f. The act of quilting; the price paid for quilting, &c.
s.f. See tag̠īr.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تغییر (taġyīr), from Arabic تَغْيِير (taḡyīr).

ety: for A. تغذية tag̠ẕiyat, fem., inf. n. ii of غذو 'to feed'
def: s.m. Food, nourishment ( = g̠iẕā).
more: (inf. ii. of غذا) s. m. Food, nourishment.

def: alternative form of تہ

ety: S. धा?
def: s.f. Plait, fold, ply ( = tah); multiplicity, perplexity.
more: s. f. A plait, fold, ply, multiplicity, perplexity.
( ताहि ) pron. (in Braj) To him, her, it.

def: s.f. Ground; site; floor; surface; bottom, underneath; foundation; depth; layer, stratum; fold, plait, ply; — real meaning or intent; hidden meaning; depth of meaning, profundity, subtleness; allusion, insinuation ( = ramz; kināya); — adj. (in comp.) Under, beneath, underground
more: n. f. 1. Surface; floor; ground; bottom. Yeh kabūtar tah kā sachchā hai . 2. Fold; plait; layer; stratum. 3. The hidden or real meaning or intent. Bāt bhī kaheṅge to tah kī. tah-bāzār . The ground or site of a market. tah-bāzārī; Pop. tah-bazārī , n. f. Ground rent of a stall in a market; a cess levied from venders at markets. tah ba tah , adv. Plait by plait; every fold. tah ba tah , adj. 1. Fold within fold; complicated; involved. 2. ( Bot .) Plicate. tah ba tah k . v. n. n. f. 1. Service; attendance; servitude; duty. 1. Sādh, saṅt kī ṭahl-kar līje kuch hu dharm, Tulsī, pher nā milegā bār bār ye janm . Tulsī Dās. Serve perfect holy men, some holiness attain, This life, O Tulsi, ne'er will you times o'er regain. 2. Bābū, pahle ṭahalyā, mātā kī karī āo, Hamro ṭahil jī se bisāro . A ballad. Sarvan. Kind sir, your service first unto my mother pay, The duty which you owe to me forget, I pray. 3. Sādh saṅt kī ṭahal ko uṭho na baiṭho jāë, Tulsī, lālach len ko dauṛā dauṛā jāë . Tulsī Dās. On perfect holy men to wait they never sit or stand, By greed possessed they run and clutch whatever comes to hand. 2. Work; business; task. 3. Housewifery; house-keeping. ṭahal, yā ṭahal ṭakorī k . v. a. To serve; drudge; attend or wait upon. Phr.—To do one's bidding; to dance attendance on. 1. Ṭahal ṭikorā ne karbā to khāëlā ke detwā? Mag. If you dont work how will you live? 2. Pahle dūdh kalas bharvāyā, pāchhe terā janam karāyā, Ṭahal karan ko dās aur dāsī, sūkhe bistar tohe sulāyā . Kabir. For milk provision first was made against your natal day, Slaves for your service ready were, on bedding dry you lay.

Jab Jam-rāj agampur āë, chhupat phirat kuṭhṛī kuṭhṛī, 'Nek chhimā karo, e Jam-rāj, ṭhaur ṭhaur merī māyā bikhrī! ' Bhajan. The king of death appears, the creature flies from cell to cell, 'O spare me yet a while, my scattered hoards let me but tell!' 3. Bahte ko bah jāne de, aur mat batlāë ṭhaur, Samjhāë se samjhe nahīṅ, to dhakke de de aur . Prov. (inf. ii. of حلّ) s. (used generally in compos. with verbs) 1. Dissolving, digestion, concoction, solubility. 2. Making lawful. تحليل هونا taḥlīl honā, To waste, to consume.
subs: v.t. To dissolve, to discuss; to digest; to assimilate; — taḥlīl-karne-wālā, adj. (in Med.) Dissolvent, discutient
v.n. To be dissolved; to waste away; to be digested.

s.f. = ṭahlūʼā; ṭahalnī; qq.v. s.m. A manager of household concerns; a servant, attendant, drudge, menial, labourer.

more: (inf. ii. of حمد) s. f. Praising God repeatedly.

s. f. A branch.

def: s.f. A small branch, a spray, twig.

adj. Inlaid (particularly, iron with gold).
n. f. ﮩن anointing; Ped. for badhāī , 4. Congratulation.

def: n. m. A push; shove (to call attention); a goad; a spur.
s. m. A blow.

v.t. To beat, thump, hammer ( = ṭhoknā); to push, shove; to impel, urge on, goad, spur.
v. a. To push; shove; goad; apply the spur to.

ety: inf. n. ii of حرف 'to alter in form,' &c.
def: s.f. Altering (words from their proper meanings); alteration; inversion, transposition (of a word or letter); forming an anagram; an anagram; mistranscription clerical error; deliberate alteration of a word so as t, change its meaning, falsification; — cutting the nib of a pen obliquely.
more: n. f. حرف altering. 1. Transposition of words or letters. 2. An anagram. 3. A clerical error; an alteration. 4. Tampering; falsification.
(inf. ii. of حرف) s. f. 1. Transposition of words or letters; an anagram. 2. Sharpening and cutting the point of a pen obliquely.

def: s.f. pl. (of taḥrīf), Alterations, changes; anagrams.

ety: inf. n. ii of حرك 'to move'
def: s.f. Putting in motion; motion, movement, agitation, commotion; incitement, stimulus, encouragement, excitement, instigation, temptation
more: n. f. حرک it moved. 1. Motion; movement. 2. Incitement; instigation; stimulation. tahrīk karnā , v. a. To urge; put up to; instigate.

(inf. ii. of حرکت) s. f. 1. Movement, agitation, incitement, stimulation, excitement, instigation, temptation, encouragement. تحريک دينا taḥrīk denā, To touch, to move, to affect, to encourage, to actuate, excite. تحريک کرنا taḥrīk karnā, To tempt.
subs: taḥrīk karnā (-kī), To move, incite (to), to urge, actuate, excite, encourage, tempt; to affect, touch.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحریک (tahrīk), from Arabic تَحْرِيك (taḥrīk).

ety: for A. تحريمة taḥrīmat, fem.; from taḥrīm, inf. n. ii of حرم 'to be prohibited,' &c.
def: s.m. The saying allāhǒ akbar, "God is great,' at the commencement of prayer; the commencement of prayer (when the person praying is prohibited, by uttering the takbīr, from saying and doing anything extraneous to prayer); — assumption of a pilgrim's garb (since this entitles the person to reverence or respect).
more: (inf. ii. of حرم q. v.) s. m. The commencement of prayer (as, worldly thoughts are then forbidden).

ety: inf. n. ii of حر 'to be hot'; 'to be free'
def: s.f. Setting at liberty, manumission; — writing elegantly and accurately; writing, description; a written statement or declaration, a document; fee for writing (anything for another); ornamental lines (on a drawing or picture), lines; pencilling, painting (e.g. surme-kī taḥrīr); — adj. Written, dated
more: n. f. حر he wrote well. 1. A writing; a written statement or declaration; minute; composition. 2. A document; deed; a bond ( tamassuk ). 3. A fee for writing anything for another. 4. Lines; tracery. 5. Pencilling; painting. Surme kī tahrīr . Mīr Hasan.

(inf. ii. of حرّ) s. f. 1. Writing correctly, writing, description. 2. Manumission. 3. adj. Written, dated. (Plur. تحارير taḥārīr ). تحريراقليدس taḥrīr-i-uḳledas, Euclidʼs elements. تحريرکرنا taḥrīr karnā, To write, describe, record. تحريرهونا taḥrīr honā, To be written, &c.
subs: tahrīr-uqlaidas, (or simply) taḥrīr, s.f. Euclid's elements
s.f. Writing between lines; interlineation: taḥrīr-ě-z̤ahrī, s.f. A writing on the back, endorsement
(-kī), To write, to describe, to record
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحریر (tahrīr) from Arabic تحرير (taḥrīr).

def: Endorsement on a bill.

ety: inf. n. ii of حرص 'to desire eagerly,' &c.
def: s.f. Making greedy; inflaming, inciting; instigation, stimulation

subs: v.t. To inflame, stimulate, to entice, allure. (N.B. The form in classical Arabic is taḥrīẓ, not taḥrīṣ.)

def: s. m. The stuffing of a saddle.

ety: inf. n. ii of حصل 'to be produced; to be realized'
def: s.f. Getting, acquiring; collecting; gain, acquisition, profit, attainment; learning; collection (particularly of revenues or rents); the revenue jurisdiction of a taḥṣīl-dār; the station or court of a taḥṣīl-dār
more: n. f. حصول became educated. 1. Acquisition; attainment; gain; profit. 2. Collection; collection of revenue. 3. The revenue jurisdiction of a tahsīldār or subordinate collector; a tahsīldār's station or court. tahsīl-dār' , n. m. A sub-collector of the Government. tahsīldār'ī , n. f. The office, duty, or jurisdiction of a sub-collector. tahsīl karnā, tahsīlnā , v. a. 1. To gain; get; acquire; attain. 2. To collect (revenue, etc.); realize; raise. tahsīl karne-wālā , n. m. One who collects revenue; a receiver of collections. tahsīl-i-mālguzārī, yā jamٴa . Collection of the revenue. tahsīl-i-nā-jāiz . Illegal collection of the revenue. haq-i-tahsīl . Commission or percentage on collections. khās tahsīl . Government collections. zar-i-tahsīl . Collections; amount of collections. qurq-i-tahsīl . Revenue attachment. kachchī, yā khām tahsīl . Collections direct from the cultivators. nisf tahsīl…

(inf. ii. of حصول) s. f. 1. Gain, acquisition, profit, attainment, learning. 2. Collection (particularly, of revenues). Pl. تحصيلات taḥṣīlāt, Collections, acquisitions. تحصيل کرنا taḥṣīl karnā, To acquire, to gain, to learn, to collect (revenue, &c.).
subs: s.f. The office, duty, or jurisdiction of a sub-collector; collectorship
(-kī), To get, gain, acquire; to attain, learn; to collect (revenue, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَحْصِیل (tahsīl), from Arabic تَحْصِيل (taḥṣīl).

s. m. A collector, a tax-gatherer.
s. f. Collectorship, tax-gathering.
v. a. 1. To collect, to gather a tax. 2. To oppress, to afflict.

ety: from taḥsīl
def: v.t. To acquire; to collect, gather (a tax = taḥṣīl karnā, q.v.s.v. taḥṣīl); to oppress, to afflict.

(inf. ii. of حسن) s. f. Approbation, applause, acclamation. تحسين تلفظ taḥsīn-i-talaffuz̤, Euphony. تحسين کرنا taḥsīn karnā, To praise, applaud, approve.

ety: in A. phrases taḥtu
def: s.m. The location which is beneath, the interior part, the under part, the inferior part, depth; the nadir; — subjection, dominion, possession, authority, control; — prep. & adv. Beneath, under; in subjection (to), under the control (of, -ke)
more: n. m. تحت beneath. 1. Charge; possession; occupancy ( ikhtyār 4, 6, 7). 2. The nadir ( pattāl ). taht-us-sarāë . Ped. Lower regions. See پاتال taht meṅ , adv. Under control; in subjection to. 2. At foot; below. taht meṅ ānā , v. n. To come under one's authority. taht meṅ rakhnā , v. n. 1. To have in one's possession; to hold; have; possess. 2. To keep under; have under command; manage. taht meṅ lānā , v. a. 1. To bring under; to bring under one's authority or control; to take possession; to occupy. 2. To bring under subjection; subject; subdue.

s. m. 1. The inferiour part, below, under. 2. Subjection, possession. تحت الثرا taḥtu-s̤-s̤arā or تحت الثري taḥtu-s̤-s̤arạ̄, s. m. Under the earth. تحت الشعاع taḥtu-sh-shuʼāﻌﻌ, s. m. Change of the moon, conjunction with the sun. تحت تصرف taḥt-i-taṣarruf or تحت فرمان taḥt-i-farmān, s. m. Subjection, dominion. تحت لفظي taḥt-lafz̤ī, adj. Literal. adv. Verbally, literal translation, interlinear translation. تحت مين لانا taḥt meṅ lānā, To conquer, subjugate, bring under, reduce.
subs: s.m. The depth of the moist earth; the under part of the earth, the nether regions
s.m. Change of the moon, conjunction with the sun
taḥt-taṣarruf, s.m. & adv. Subjection, dominion, authority, control; — under the dominion (of, -ke), &c.
s.m. & adv. Jurisdiction, control, &c. = taḥt-ě-taṣarruf, q.v.
adj. & adv. Literal, verbal (as a translation); — literally, verbally
adv. Under, under control or authority (of, -ke), in subjection (to); below, at the foot (of a page, &c.)
(-ke), To come under the authority or control (of)
v.t. To keep under, maintain in subjection, to control; to have or keep in (one's) possession, to possess, hold, have
v.t. To bring under, to reduce to subjection, to conquer, subdue; to bring under control or authority; to take possession of.

s.m. Cash-keeper, cashier, treasurer (esp. in a provincial treasury)

s. f. The office of cash-keeper.

more: (inf. ii. of هذب) s. f. Purifying, adjusting, adorning, refinement, edification. تـﮩذيب اخلاق tahẕīb-i-aḵẖlāḳ, Civilization, good breeding.

(in Braj) adj. So many. تي بير tai ber, So often.

def: E. n. f. From H. tavā , q. v. An earthen frying pan.

more: n. m. طوي folded. A fold; plait. taë k. , v. a. 1. To roll up; fold. 2. To pass across or over; traverse. 3. To close; dispose of ( chukānā ).

def: s.m. Expressed oil (prepared from sesamum, mustard-seed, &c.); storax, gum, benzoin, incense

s.m. Marriage presents, consisting of oil, cocoanuts, &c., sent by the bridegroom to the bride shortly before his own arrival to celebrate the marriage
adj. As black as an oiled baking-pan; glossy black
(-par), To anoint the head, shoulders, and hands and feet of the bride and bridegroom with oil mixed with turmeric during the marriage ceremony; (hence) the marriage ceremony above described

ety: Prk. थाणि or थाणे; S. स्थाने
def: postpn. (governing gen. with -ke), To, up to: (-ke taʼīṅ = ko; e.g. uske taʼiṅ = usko; apne taʼīṅ, = āp-ko or apne-āp-ko).
more: postpos. Up to, to, at. (Or denoting after a genitive form, the objective case).

def: prep. To; up to; at. It denotes the objective case, and is generally used with the possessive apne , in the sense of self, as apne taīṅ oneself.

ety: act. part. of طعن 'to pierce, stab' (with a spear, or with the tongue
def: s.m. A reviler, an asperser, a slanderer, calumniator.
more: (part. act. of طعن) s. m. An asperser, slanderer, reproacher.

ety: corr. of taʻaiyun, or of taʻyīn; but in the latter case it should be fem.
def: s.m. Appointing; appointment; deputation (see next)
more: n. m. عين appointed. Theact of appointing; determination; settlement; establishment; appointment. taaīyun k . v. n. To fix; determine; establish; institute.
(inf. v. of عين) s. m. Manifestation, appointing, appointment, establishing, establishment, assigning, deputing.
(for تعيين inf. ii. of عين) s. m. Appointing, deputation, appointment. تعين کرنا taʼīﻌn karnā, To appoint.

ety: act. part. of طير 'to fly'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Flying; — a bird, a winged creature
subs: s.m. 'The bird of the lote-tree,' a name of the angel Gabriel.

ety: inf. n. of طير 'to fly'
def: s.m. Flying; — a flying thing, a winged creature, a bird, &c. (syn. t̤āʼir).
more: s. m. 1. Flying. 2. A bird. See طاير t̤āʼịr. (Pers. plur. طيران Birds).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَیْر (tayr), from Arabic طَيْر (ṭayr).

def: n. m. طير to fly. S. tri to cross. A bird (پرنده 1).
more: part. act. (of طير) 1. Flying. 2. s. m. A bird. (Pers. plur. طايران).

def: s. (dakh.) Clap (noise: Harris ).

ety: rt. of tairnā, q.v.+āk; Prk. आवको = S. आपि+अकः(आपकः)
def: s.m. A swimmer ( = pairāk, q.v.). s.m. A swimmer ( = tairāk).
more: n. m. A swimmer. tairā'k'ī, pairākī , n. f. The art of swimming.
wety: From تَیْرنا (tairnā, “to swim”).

ety: tairāk+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The art of swimming ( = pairākī).
oblique plural of تَیْراک (tairāk)
airport, airfield
aerial; bird's-eye (of a view)
wety: From طائِر (tāir, “bird; winged creature”) + ـَانَہ (-āna).

ety: tair˚ or tīr˚ = Prk. तर(इ) or तीर(इ) = S. तर(ति) or तिर(ति), rt. तॄ
def: v.n. To swim; to float (syn. pairnā); — v.t. To swim over or across; to go through, become familiar with, to master (a business, or a subject, &c.)
more: v. n. S. तरणं 1. To swim (پيرنا); to float. Tair ke āī hai bahte daryāo ko! ( Kakṛī cucumber). Delhī street cry. 2. To be well versed in; to know thoroughly. Yeh is kām meṅ khūb taire huë haiṅ .

v. a. 1. To call; call to; holla. 2. To speak; recite; sing. 3. To bawl; scream; roar. Ṭer ṭer-ke robe, apnī lāj khobe . Rus. Prov. Aloud she cries, her shame out flies. 4. To spend, pass (one's days). Umar ṭeṛ ho gaī .
v. a. To swim, to cross over.
v. (dakh.) To swim, to float ( Harris ).
subs: (-meṅ), To be well versed (in), be familiar (with).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀢𑀺𑀭𑀤𑀺 (*tiradi), from Sanskrit तरति (tarati, “crosses over”).

def: s.m. A he-goat; a buck; a stag.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. A goat (especially, an old goat).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀻𑀲 (tīsa), from Sanskrit त्रिंशत् (triṃśat).
